observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to create a PPE Inventory
Committee in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31,
2020, and direct them to:
1.9.1 Prepare and submit the PIP for the conduct of a physical count of
inventories of the LGU; and
1.9.2 Expedite its procedures in conducting a physical count of inventories, and
timely submit the required reports such as the RPCPPE, ICF, List of
PPEs found at Station and List of Non-existing/Missing PPEs, PIP.

We also recommended and the Office of the Municipal Accountant agreed to
collaborate with the PPE Inventory Committee to ensure that all PPEs
included in the RPCPPE are duly reconciled with the accounting records.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to revise the Notes to
the Financial Statements to include the principal amount, interest rate,
maturity date, amount for each purpose, and interest expense of the Loans
Payable – Domestic account, to properly inform the users of the financial
statements, the financial performance of the Municipal Government,
particularly in financial management.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to ensure that the cash
accountabilities of AOs are in accordance with their existing bonds, pursuant to
Treasury Circular No. 02-2019.

We also recommended that Management review and evaluate the maximum
accountabilities of AOs and apply for an increase in the amount of bond, when
necessary, in accordance with RA No. 7160 and Treasury Circular No. 02-
2019.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to, henceforth:
4.8.1 Require the outgoing AO to submit their cashbook to the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant for verification and recording
purposes;
4.8.2 Instruct the newly appointed or designated AO to use a new cashbook in
accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002; and
4.8.3 Brief the newly appointed or designated AOs on the proper recording of
transactions and other matters relating to their role as a disbursing
officer.

We recommended and the AO agreed to, henceforth, record transactions and
reconcile the balances per book and cash on hand daily, in accordance with
COA Circular No. 97-002. We also recommended and the Municipal
Accountant agreed to ensure that newly appointed/designated AOs are briefed
on the proper recording of the transactions and other matters related to their
work.

We recommended and Management agreed to include in its annual budget the
appropriations for the purchase and installation of a safety vault or a steel
cabinet with a vault for the DO and the provision of additional security features
at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office. This measure is necessary to exercise
prudence and ensure the safety of government funds and important financial
documents, in accordance with Sections 2 and 123 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and Management agreed to stop designating JOs as field
collectors and strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 7.3 of the COA-DBM
JC No. 2, s. 2020.

We further recommended that Management fill the vacant positions in the
Treasurer’s Office and regularly review and assess the effectiveness of the
current staffing structure to ensure optimal performance and compliance.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare and
submit to the Audit Team the monthly BRS pursuant to Sections 3.2 and 3.4
of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer agreed to closely coordinate and regularly reconcile their respective
records for early detection and correction of adjustments and errors, if any,
pursuant to Section 74 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and the Municipal Health Officer (MHO) and the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman agreed to, henceforth, use the latest
DPRI as the ceiling price in the procurement of drugs and medicines in
compliance with RA No. 9502 and DOH AO No. 2019-0040 dated October 14,
2019.

We recommended and the BAC agreed to ensure the indication of complete
detailed specifications in the PO before proceeding with the delivery of drugs
and medicines. We also recommended and the personnel concerned or the
end-user duly authorized by the Municipal Mayor, as Head of the Procuring
Entity, agreed to fill up the relevant and required information in the PO
pursuant to Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We recommended and Management agreed to immediately cease or amend
its Contract of Service to ensure that the hired personnel does not have
control and supervision over regular employees under the Municipal
Government.

Otherwise, we recommended and the said personnel agreed to obtain the
necessary qualifications, before applying for the MHO position in the
Municipality of Siaton.

We recommended and Management agreed to immediately stop providing
RATA and other allowances and benefits to personnel hired under the
Contract of Service.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
13.9.1 Include in its annual budget the appropriation for the establishment of
IAS; and
13.9.2 Facilitate the creation of an IAS in the Municipality, in accordance with
AO No. 70 and RA No. 3456, as amended, following up the approval
from the Sangguniang Panlalawigan of the Province of Negros Oriental.

We also recommended that the IAS use the 2020 Revised PGIAM in the
exercise of their internal audit functions.

We recommended and the Municipal Assessor agreed to:
14.14.1 Coordinate with the Provincial Assessor in the preparation of the
updated SMV of the Municipality pursuant to Section 212 of RA
No. 7160; and
14.14.2 Initiate the immediate conduct of a GRRPA after the enactment of
the updated SMVs and ensure that this is done every three years as
required under Section 219 of RA No. 7160 and DILG-DOF JMC
No. 2010-001.

We recommended and the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) agreed to:
18.9.1 Ensure that budget allocation for GAD is optimally utilized in providing
adequate facilities and services, to address gender issues and the GAD
mandate of the LGU;
18.9.2 Prepare a more realistic and attainable GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to
ensure that GAD projects and activities will be carried out in accordance
with the approved plan; and
18.9.3 Establish an effective monitoring and evaluation system to monitor the
implementation of the annual GPB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management improve its internal control system on asset management, by ensuring that all Property issued are properly documented, duly receipted and recorded. We further recommend compliance with existing rules and regulations on supplies and property.
observations.managementAction: There was a lack of personnel to focus on the Stock Card preparation and maintenance. However, the Management planned to assign a staff for this particular task.
observations.reason: There was a lack of personnel to focus on the Stock Card preparation and maintenance.

observations.recommendation:
a) Design/adopt measures and strategies to remedy the high expenditures in the operations of the Market and Slaughterhouse.
b) Review and revisit the Plans and Targets in the operations of these economic enterprises in order to address the areas where the municipal government can generate additional income and minimize costs/expenses.
c) Study possible options or remedies in order to rescind the contract with the private contractor in the operation of the comfort rooms in the market that will be beneficial to the government.
observations.managementAction: Management did not consider this a priority in CY 2021.
observations.reason: Management did not consider this a priority in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare a 5-Year Business Plan and request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass an ordinance recognizing the continued operations of the existing LEEs in accordance with the LEE Manual as prescribed in Local Budget Circular No. 111 dated June 10, 2016 to ensure the efficient and effective provision of basic services and facilities to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Management did not find these requirements regarding the operation of LEEs a priority during the year under audit.
observations.reason: Management did not find these requirements a priority during the year under audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should enter into a MOA between the Municipality of Siaton and the PCL NOC to ensure that the financial assistance is utilized in accordance with the powers and functions of the League pursuant to Section 498 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: PCL members in 2018, during the granting of financial assistance, were no longer in service except for a few. They explained that they did not know who to approach to help them in the drafting of a MOA since the PCL Negros Oriental has a new set of officers.
observations.reason: PCL members in 2018, during the granting of financial assistance, were no longer in service except for a few; they did not know who to approach to help them in the drafting of a MOA since the PCL Negros Oriental has a new set of officers.

observations.recommendation:
a) Require the Municipal Treasurer to demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support the release of financial aid to the latter.
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor the utilization of the funds released to these Leagues by requiring the recipient Leagues concerned to submit liquidation reports. In this manner, the propriety and validity of the expenditures could be properly evaluated.
c) Require the Leagues concerned to submit a copy of their Constitution and By-Laws as basis of reviewing bodies in determining the propriety of the fund release.
observations.managementAction: The LMP and PCL did not have Official Receipts of the Republic of the Philippines; they issued only BIR registered Official Receipts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal officials consider officially donating the multicabs to the respective barangays in actual custody thereof, covered by Deeds of Donation clearly stating the intended use of the vehicle and imposing the condition that the vehicle shall be used solely for the purpose stated.
observations.managementAction: On-going coordination of concerned Municipal Officials with the barangays regarding the use of the vehicles.
observations.reason: Coordination with the barangays regarding the use of the vehicles is on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a PPE Inventory
Committee that will focus on the conduct of physical count of properties and complete
the same within three months, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the PPE
Inventory to prepare the PIP and submit the required reports such as the RCPPE,
ICF, List of PPEs found at Station and List of Non-existing/Missing PPEs.

We furthermore recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant
collaborate with the PPE Inventory Committee to ensure that all PPEs included in
the RCPPE are duly reconciled with the accounting records.

We recommended that Management plan on programs and activities which
could utilize the Municipality’s share of the Lotto Charity Fund, such as health
programs, medical assistance and services, and charities of national character, and
implement the same according to EO No. 357 dated August 5, 1996 and RA No. 1169,
as amended.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee require the personnel
concerned or the end-user duly authorized by the Municipal Mayor, as Head of the
Procuring Entity, to fill out the relevant and required information in the PO pursuant
to Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We further recommended that the BAC refrain from awarding a contract unless the
RFQ contains the required details of the goods to be procured.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan enact an Ordinance
establishing the Scholarship Program of the Municipality and, henceforth, implement
the same. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a Scholarship
Committee which will establish the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of the
program, including the criteria, conditions of the scholarship and responsibilities of
the Scholarship Committee, as well as the scholars.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), henceforth,
require the certification from the requisitioning officer or by duly authorized officer
that the products being requisitioned and procured fall within and conform with the
PNF Essential Medicines List to support the Purchase Request, in accordance with
the aforementioned DOH Administrative Order.

We recommended that Management and the BAC thoroughly examine the
documents submitted by the suppliers to ensure that contracts are awarded to
suppliers with financial capability and that goods delivered to the Municipality are
reasonably priced.

We further recommended that Management and BAC resort to Negotiated
Procurement-Emergency Cases only when necessary and the conditions provided in
the IRR of R.A. 9184 and NMP 003-2020 are present.

We recommended that the BAC carefully examine the eligibility requirements of contractors, particularly their PCAB licenses to ensure that bidders of various infrastructure projects are authorized to engage in the project to be implemented.
observations.managementAction: For the audited year, BAC had already carefully examined their contractors’ PCAB licenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from disbursing funds without appropriations in accordance with Section 305(a) of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Management had already submitted the approved appropriation ordinance for the Bayanihan Grant with an explanation/justification for late approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the BAC to submit the documents which are lacking, particularly the approved Performance Specifications and Parameters and the Construction Safety and Health Program, the Proposed Design and Construction Schedule and the Utility Locations, and to henceforth ensure that the required documents for preliminary design and construction studies required under Item No. 7 of Annex G of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 are compiled before conducting the bidding and award of design and build contracts.
observations.managementAction: had already submitted the lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that BAC conduct the eligibility screening and post-qualification with utmost care in order to ensure that the contract is awarded only to qualified contractors who have the technical capability to implement the project in accordance with the standard of the government set under RA No. 9184 and its RIRR.
observations.managementAction: This had been disallowed in audit due to the contractor’s PCAB qualifications which did not meet the necessary qualification for the project. Disallowed and pending appeal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require Molrow Construction and Supply to prepare and submit the required detailed engineering studies for the Design and Build of Two-Story Multi-Purpose Building and Other Facilities as mandated in Annex A of 2016 Revised IRR of R.A No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC was already submitted the Value Analysis and Engineering.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify collection of real property taxes and avail of the remedies provided under Sections 254 and 258 of the Local Government Code, especially for those with delinquent accounts of more than two years.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had already sent demand letters to the delinquent taxpayers, informing them of enforcing remedies under the law for non-payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the errors of the affected accounts and require the immediate liquidation of the funds transferred.
observations.managementAction: PNP Regional Office No. 18 and Brgy. Giligaon had already submitted to the Municipal Accountant certifications utilizing the transferred funds. The Municipal Accountant had already made the necessary adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and BAC strictly adhere to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: BAC was already following the provisions of RA 9184 in the procurement of infrastructure projects and heavy equipment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open dump site, concretized methods and procedures for the phase-out/eventual closure of the existing open dump site within a specific time frame and hasten its full compliance with Republic Act 9003.
observations.managementAction: The open dumpsite for solid waste was already closed and a sanitary landfill at Brgy. Napacao was already identified and approved by the DENR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit the accomplishment reports of those projects recorded under the Construction in Progress accounts to the Municipal Accountant and take up the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of related accounts.
observations.managementAction: All purchases of construction materials were now lodged in the Construction Materials Inventory and will be closed to the appropriate asset account upon the receipt of the 100% accomplishment reports.

observations.recommendation:
a) Submit immediately all the required documents that were not submitted/attached to the disbursement voucher together with an explanation for its non-compliance.
b) Explain the failure to conduct the proper pre and post-qualification evaluations otherwise, if same was undertaken, submit the report duly signed by the BAC and TWG members.
c) Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations, Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 which enumerates/provides for the basic requirements in the procurement through public bidding.
d) Issue the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment to the officer/employee accountable for the unit.
e) The BAC should observe the governing principles of:
• Transparency in the procurement process and in the implementation of the procurement contract;
• Competitiveness by extending equal opportunity to enable private contracting parties who are eligible and qualified to participate in public bidding; and
• Fairness in dealing with the participating bidders as well as in the awarding of the contract.
observations.managementAction: The BAC had already submitted the enumerated documents. The ARE was issued to the Municipal Engineer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) require the personnel concerned or the end-use duly authorized by the Municipal Mayor, as Head of the Procuring Entity, to fill up the relevant and required information in the PO pursuant to Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996. We also recommended that the BAC refrain from awarding a contract when the RFQ does not contain specific details of the goods procured.
observations.managementAction: BAC had religiously ensured these – Some transactions were followed. Some transactions had already been completed upon the advice of the Audit Team, hence those transactions still did not conform to the recommendation.
observations.reason: Some transactions had already been completed upon the advice of the Audit Team, hence those transactions still did not conform to the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the personnel responsible for the distribution of food supplies and testing kits to immediately submit the list of recipients to the Office of the Municipal Accountant as basis for the preparation of the JEV to record the expenses.
observations.managementAction: The Management had already submitted partial – Distribution Lists for the purchases of rice, medicines, antigen kits, and others were not yet submitted by the concerned employees. The Municipal Accountant constantly reminded the concerned employees to submit the distribution lists.
observations.reason: Distribution Lists for the purchases of rice, medicines, antigen kits, and others were not yet submitted by the concerned employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan appropriate funds for the titling of lands and the Municipal Assessor be directed to exert effort in titling all parcels of land owned by the municipality. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain complete accounting records of these properties and the Municipal Treasurer keep an organized filing of land titles, tax declarations, deed of donations and other related documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had appropriated funds for the continuation of the tedious land title processing and the documentary requirements for lots that date back to the mid-1900s. During 2022, Management had submitted complete documentary requirements for 7 lots to DENR-Dumaguete for titling and final review.
observations.reason: Due to the long and tedious land title processing and the lack of necessary documentary requirements for lots that date back to the mid-1900s.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to effect the immediate reconciliation of the cash accounts between general ledger and cashbooks and to affect the monthly closing of the books only after the two records are reconciled.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer commenced reconciling the cashbook and journals for the CYs 2020 and 2021. The Municipal Accountant promised to continue the reconciliation on prior years. Management had already filled up a vacant position in the Accounting Office, a factor to keep the reconciliation going.
observations.reason: Due to the voluminous work of the Accounting Office, not much time could be allotted to do the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit to the Auditor bank reconciliation statements monthly pursuant to Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011.
observations.managementAction: Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements had been prepared but were constantly delayed in submission of these reports.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to concentrate on the preparation of bank statements caused the delayed submission of these reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit copies of the perfected contracts/purchase orders/job orders and the related supporting documents within five (5) working days from its execution in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Copies of the POs and JOs were usually submitted to COA as supporting disbursement vouchers (DVs). Management found it hard to submit the POs and JOs within five (5) days from the date of perfection since the end-users usually prepare, process and serve the POs and JOs to the suppliers, and will furnish the Accounting Office and the Treasurer’s Office only upon payment processing which is usually beyond five (5) days from the date of execution.
observations.reason: Management found it hard to submit the POs and JOs within five (5) days from the date of perfection since the end-users usually prepare, process and serve the POs and JOs to the suppliers, and will furnish the Accounting Office and the Treasurer’s Office only upon payment processing which is usually beyond five (5) days from the date of execution.

observations.recommendation:
a) Required the refund of the 2012 CNA Incentive and the Anniversary Bonus paid to the officers and employees of LGU-Siaton.
b) Henceforth, observe the regulations in the determination of savings and payments of the incentive and bonuses as outlined under DBM Budget Circular No. 2012-4 dated December 17, 2012 and Administrative Order No. 263 dated March 28, 1996, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The employees who retired/resigned from service had fully refunded the disallowed benefits.
observations.reason: Not all employees had fully refunded the disallowed benefits.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management and the BAC examine thoroughly the documents submitted by the suppliers to ensure that contracts are awarded to suppliers with financial capability and goods delivered to the Municipality are reasonably priced.
observations.managementAction: BAC religiously ensured these recommendations were followed. Prior to the Audit Team’s recommendations, some transactions were already completed. Moving forward, the BAC now requires the submission of the suppliers' audited financial statements and their NFCCs.
observations.reason: Prior to the Audit Team’s recommendations, some transactions were already completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC to provide the Municipal Accountant documents pertaining to the acquisition of heavy equipment. Moreover, Management also agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to secure documents from the DBP and draw a journal entry to record the acquisition of the heavy equipment to fairly present the Property, Plant and Equipment account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office had already exerted all efforts to comply with this. The Municipal Accountant agreed to sit with the bank personnel assigned to the Loans Division to sort these things out.
observations.reason: With the volume of transactions the Accountant has the same number of staff since 1994, making it hard to comply; she had already requested the creation of additional positions in her office, approval of which depends upon the availability of funds.

observations.managementAction: Due to the COVID-19 pandemic, the Management had not taken any step to continue the physical count of all PPEs. The Inventory Committee was not able to continue the conduct of physical count.
observations.reason: In CY 2021, the Municipality was hardly hit by the pandemic. The number of employees infected increased, resulting in several lockdowns and the quarantine of employees at home. Hence, the Inventory Committee was not able to continue the conduct of physical count.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management notify the concerned individuals or entities to settle their accounts or initiate appropriate actions to recover the amount. We also recommend that management conduct a periodic review and monitoring of receivable accounts which are not collected or recovered within the prescribed period to determine the validity of the amount recorded as assets in the books of accounts.

observations.reason: The Office of the Municipal Accountant found it difficult to find and retrieve the source documents especially the KKK receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management demand from PNP RO 7 the liquidation documents of the current and prior year’s fund transfers as required in Item No. 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012 as amended by COA Circular No. 2016-02 dated May 16, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Management, particularly the Municipal Accountant, had already demanded liquidation from the PNP RO 7.
observations.reason: The PNP Region VII still had not submitted the required liquidation documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned officials refund the increases in RATA they have received in CY 2013 totaling ₱703,800.00.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials who retired/resigned from the year had fully refunded the disallowed RATA increase upon retirement/resignation from service.
observations.reason: The remaining concerned officials had already refunded upon retirement/resignation from service.

observations.recommendation:
a) All employees be required to refund the PEI of ₱10,000.00 each or a total of ₱1,709,000.00.
b) The provisions of Budget Circular No. 2013-3 be strictly observed.
c) The LGU must exercise prudence in the use of local funds so that the delivery of basic services and developments to the public will not be adversely hampered/affected.
observations.managementAction: The employees who retired/resigned from service had fully refunded the disallowed benefits.

observations.recommendation:
a) Adopt measures to ensure the immediate implementation or liquidation of the financial assistance from the National and Local Government upon the completion of the intended purposes.
b) Return any unused balances upon completion of the project/program. Otherwise, if there are priority projects that need additional funding, request for authority to use the balances.
c) For unimplemented projects with huge fund allocation, evaluate whether the program/activities are still needed, if so, pursue its implementation as soon as possible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) Require accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances to immediately submit the approved liquidation documents. If the cash advances are no longer needed, require the officers to refund the whole amount or the unused portion, as the case may be. Failure of the AO to liquidate his cash advance within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause for the withholding of his salary as provided for under paragraphs 9.2 and 9.3 of COA Circular 97-002 and the institution of other sanctions provided for under COA Circular 2012-004; b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to closely implement and monitor the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, send final demand letters separately to each accountable officer to remind the AOs of their outstanding cash advances and their liquidation. Stop from granting additional cash advance to officers and employees with outstanding cash advance; c) Direct the accounting personnel who is in charge of maintaining the subsidiary records of the account “Advances to Officers and Employees” to regularly notify the municipal accountant of any outstanding cash advance of accountable officers to preclude the latter from processing and obligating additional cash advance; and d) Impose the sanction of withholding of salaries of incumbent officials and employees with long-outstanding cash advances as provided under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office regularly monitored the granting of advances to officers and employees and subsequent liquidations. Management demanded regular liquidation from employees and the NGAs but had not received any.
observations.reason: Management demanded regular liquidation from other employees and the NGAs but had not received any.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) Submit immediately all the required documents that were not submitted/attached to disbursement voucher together with an explanation for its non-compliance. b) Explain the failure to conduct the proper pre and post-qualification evaluations otherwise, if same was undertaken, submit the report duly signed by the BAC and TWG members. c) Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations, Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 which enumerates/provides for the basic requirements in the procurement through public bidding. d) Issue the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment to the officer/employee accountable for the unit. e) The BAC should observe the governing principles of: I. Transparency in the procurement process and in the implementation of the procurement contract; II. Competitiveness by extending equal opportunity to enable private contracting parties who are eligible and qualified to participate in public bidding; and f) Fairness in dealing with the participating bidders as well as in the awarding of the contract.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to correct the errors of the affected accounts and require
the immediate liquidation of the funds transferred.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize
liabilities upon receipt of goods rather than upon payment pursuant to Section 4(s)
of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the BAC agreed to use shopping as mode of
procurement only when it satisfies the requirements of Section 52.1 of the Revised
IRR of R.A 9184.

We recommended and management agreed to avoid putting the names,
initials, and/or pictures of government officials in any government properties,
programs or projects pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-101 dated
September 23, 2010.

We recommended and management agreed to prepare a 5-Year Business
Plan and request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass an ordinance recognizing the
continued operations of the existing LEEs in accordance with the LEE Manual as
prescribed in Local Budget Circular No. 111 dated June 10, 2016 to ensure the
efficient and effective provision of basic services and facilities to its constituents.

We recommended and management agreed to require the municipal drivers
to properly accomplish the driver’s trip tickets in accordance with Sections V of
COA Circular No. 75-6 dated November 7, 1975.

We recommended and management agreed to, henceforth, ensure that the net
take home pay of employees should not be lower than the amount as set forth by the
GAA for the fiscal year.

We recommended that management fully comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to promote transparency and accountability and to secure the right of the people to information on matters of public concern. Henceforth, management may request the assistance of the barangay officials concerned to take security measures to prevent the signboards from being stolen.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the AAR Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer return immediately the remaining Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) of the Municipality totaling ₱85,455.28 to the National Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has transferred the expended balance already.

observations.recommendation:
1. Require the LDRRMC to submit a LDRRMFIP for the DRRM programs annually using the prescribed format in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE for CY 2014 to Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books. She should submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month and change the description of account 231 from Fire Fighting Equipment and Accessories to Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment.
observations.managementAction: LDRRMFIP was submitted to DILG only.
observations.reason: For compliance in the ensuing year.

observations.managementAction: No cash advances were granted unless the previous cash advances were liquidated. Demand letters were sent to concerned officials and employees in February 2013. Partial refund were liquidated. As of December 31, 2016, total unliquidated cash advances amounted to ₱153,166.01.
observations.reason: Partial refund ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
a. Submit immediately all the bidding required documents that were not submitted/attached to the disbursement voucher together with an explanation for its non-compliance.
b. Explain the failure to conduct the proper pre and post-qualification evaluations otherwise, if same was undertaken, submit the report duly signed by the BAC and TWG members.
c. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations, Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 which enumerate/provide for the basic requirements in the procurement through public bidding.
d. Issue the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment to the officer/employee accountable for the unit.
e. The BAC should observe the governing principles of: i. Transparency in the procurement process and in the implementation of the procurement contract; ii. Competitiveness by extending equal opportunity to enable private contracting parties who are eligible and qualified to participate in public bidding; and f. Fairness in dealing with the participating bidders as well as in the awarding of the contract.
observations.managementAction: Documents have been submitted to COA subject for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the AAR concerned officials refund the increases in RATA they have received in CY 2013 totaling P703,800.00.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials refunded the increases in RATA they have received in CY 2013 gradually through monthly salary deduction.

observations.recommendation:
a. All employees be required to refund the PEI of P10,000.00 in each or a total of P1,709,000.00.
b. The provisions of Budget Circular No. 2013-3 be strictly observed.
The LGU must exercise prudence in the use of local funds so that the delivery of basic services and developments to the public will not be adversely hampered/affected.
observations.managementAction: The concerned employees refunded the amount collected through monthly salary deductions.

observations.recommendation:
a. Required the refund of the CNA Incentive and the Anniversary Bonus paid to the officers and employees of LGU-Siaton.
b. Henceforth, observe the regulations in the determination of savings and payments of the incentive and bonuses as outlined under DBM Budget Circular No. 2012-4 dated December 17, 2012 and Administrative Order No. 263 dated March 28, 1996, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The concerned employees refunded the amount collected through monthly salary deductions.

observations.recommendation:
a. Submit immediately all the required documents to support the disbursement vouchers covering the purchase of the backhoe excavator and rescue ambulance together with an explanation for non-compliance to the circular.
b. The Mayor explain the failure to conduct the proper pre and post-qualification evaluations, otherwise if same was undertaken, submit the report duly signed by the BAC and TWG members.
c. The Mayor explain the absence of public bidding in the procurement of the backhoe excavator and rescue ambulance.
d. The concerned municipal officials such as the Mayor, Accountant, BAC members henceforth comply with the procedural and documentary requirements of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations, Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 which enumerate/provide the basic requirements and procedures in the procurement of goods through public bidding.
e. The Municipal Treasurer require the accomplishment of Property Acknowledgement Receipts by the officers/employees accountable for the backhoe excavator and rescue ambulance.
The LDRRMC strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 84 and 21 of Republic Act No. 10121 regarding LDRRMF and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 which provides for the accounting and reporting guidelines for LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Documents were already submitted to COA subject for evaluation.

observations.recommendation:
a. Henceforth, regularly prepare and submit the Trial Balances, Financial Statements and its supporting schedules and accounts every end of quarter to the auditor concerned.
b. Implement the reshuffling scheme in order to augment the personnel assigned to the Accounting Office.
observations.managementAction: Year-end trial balances and financial statements were submitted on time but the quarterly trial balances were not up to date.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation:
a. Adopt measures to ensure the immediate implementation or liquidation of the financial assistance from the National and Local Government upon completion of the intended purposes.
b. To return any unused balances upon completion of the project/program. Otherwise, if there are priority projects that need additional funding, request for authority to use the balances.
For unimplemented projects with huge fund allocation, evaluate whether the program/activities are still needed, if so, pursue its implementation as soon as possible.
observations.managementAction: Liquidation reports were prepared and submitted to the auditor.

observations.recommendation:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support the release of financial aid to the latter.
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor the utilization of the funds released to these Leagues by requiring the recipient Leagues concerned to submit liquidation reports. In this manner, the propriety and validity of the expenditures could be properly evaluated.
c. Require the Leagues concerned to submit a copy of their Constitution and By-Laws as basis of reviewing bodies in determining the propriety of the fund release.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality no longer released funds to leagues except those provided under Republic Act No. 7160.

observations.recommendation:
a. Design/adopt measures and strategies to remedy the high expenditures in the operations of the Market and Slaughterhouse.
b. Review and revisit the Plans and Targets in the operations of these economic enterprises in order to address the areas where the municipal government can generate additional income and minimize costs/expenses.
c. Study possible options/remedies in order to rescind the contract with the private contractor in the operation of the comfort rooms in the market that will be beneficial to the government.
observations.managementAction: New tax ordinance has been implemented.

observations.recommendation: Required the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to effect the immediate reconciliation of the cash accounts between general ledger and cashbooks and to effect the monthly closing of the books only after the two records are reconciled.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation was started in 2010 but discontinued.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management notify the concerned individuals or entities to settle their accounts or initiate appropriate actions to recover the amounts. Request authority from the proper officials to write-off receivables which are deemed uncollectible and conduct a periodic review and monitoring of receivable accounts which are not collected or recovered within the prescribed period of time to determine the validity of the amount recorded as assets in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to concerned individuals or entities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management improve its internal control system on asset management, by ensuring that all property issued are properly documented, duly receipted and recorded. We further recommend compliance with existing rules and regulations on supplies and property.
observations.managementAction: No stock cards and property cards were maintained yet. However, Requisition Issue Slips (RIS) were already used to record property issuances.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.
